changes.before: and the land still gave itself away.
changes.after: and every fence line showed it.

changes.before: Jaina was not here, and the absence no longer carried irritation in Sylvanas's head. It carried cost.
changes.after: Jaina was not here, and the absence carried cost.

changes.before: The stakes pointed outward. The cavalry lanes left room for a charge. Healers sat near the middle, where men could be hauled in from an open fight. Bread wagons stood close to the kitchens, tarped against rain. Steam ghosted up beyond them.
changes.after: Stakes outward. Cavalry lanes wide enough for a charge—healers near the middle, close enough to haul men back from an open fight. Bread wagons tarped by the kitchens. Steam beyond them, thin and sour.

changes.before: That landed. Not understanding. Weight. Enough of it showed in Falric's face that she knew rumor had beaten the formal brief.
changes.after: Not understanding. Weight. Enough of it showed in Falric's face—rumor had beaten the formal brief.
Something moved behind Falric's eyes—the reflex of a man deciding whether to escalate.

changes.before: The room bent toward Arthas's urgency. It steadied around Uther's silence.
changes.after: Every officer at the table leaned toward Arthas. Uther said nothing, and nobody moved to fill it.

changes.feedbackRef: narrator-seep-guide.md → A1; negation-addiction-hunting-guide.md → Pattern 5; intent-smear-agency-laundering-guide.md → P5
changes.before: There it was. The speed of him. No pause spent on whether belief ought to hurt. He kept asking questions, good ones, and too few. Where the grain moved fastest. Which holdings mattered. Whether the Cult favored roads, storehouses, or mills. She answered cleanly. Each answer sharpened him and took one more layer of caution out of the room.
changes.after: The speed of him. He kept asking questions, good ones, and too few. Where the grain moved fastest. Which holdings mattered. Whether the Cult favored roads, storehouses, or mills. She answered cleanly. With each answer he sharpened, and the officers stopped qualifying their questions.
Uther's hands lifted from the table edge and hung at his sides, empty.

changes.before: A kingdom drove itself toward the answer it wanted least.
He had spent half his life dragging boys back from the kinds of certainty they mistook for courage.
changes.after: He had spent half his life pulling boys back from the calls that would cost them everything.

changes.before: Uther there as well, because love and duty had both failed him and he had no third thing left.
changes.after: Uther there as well, because he could not save Arthas from himself and could not walk away.

changes.before: North waited under mist, stone, and an island keep Jaina had entered alone because they had split the work sensibly and been paying for it in blood ever since.
changes.after: North lay under mist, stone, and an island keep Jaina had entered alone.

changes.before: So did every choice already made.
Caer Darrow waited somewhere beyond with its island walls and whatever had swallowed Jaina whole enough that nobody had seen her come back out.
changes.after: Caer Darrow sat somewhere beyond with its island walls and whatever had swallowed Jaina whole enough that nobody had seen her come back out.

changes.before: South, the fuse burned. North, the hand that lit it reached for the only thing in this country she still trusted not to break.
